data.title: [dso-setup/merge_ci_workflow]: merges plugin Python-stack CI jobs into NextJS template ci.yml -> fresh installs ship broken CI (mypy/test-unit-agents/testmon jobs referencing nonexistent app/poetry.lock)
data.description:
## Incident Overview

dso-setup.sh merge_ci_workflow blindly appends plugin-internal CI jobs into the target project's .github/workflows/ci.yml regardless of project stack.

## Observed
Fresh `create-dso-app` install of NextJS template:
- Template ships clean 101-line ci.yml with 3 jobs: fast-gate, tests, build
- Post-install ci.yml is 892 lines with 14 jobs including: mypy, persistence-check, test-unit-agents, test-unit-services, test-unit-api, test-unit-core, coverage-check, test-integration-hermetic, test-integration-external
- These jobs reference app/, poetry.lock, testmon, mypy — none of which exist in a NextJS project
- Every CI run on a fresh create-dso-app project will fail

## Expected
merge_ci_workflow should either (a) be stack-aware and only merge jobs applicable to the target stack, (b) merge only stack-neutral jobs (security-scan, shellcheck) and leave stack-specific jobs alone, or (c) require explicit opt-in for plugin CI merge.

## Reproducer
bash <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra/HEAD/scripts/create-dso-app.sh) my-project
diff <(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/navapbc/digital-service-orchestra-nextjs-template/HEAD/.github/workflows/ci.yml) my-project/.github/workflows/ci.yml

## Severity
P1 — every create-dso-app install produces a project that fails CI on first push.
